title: Safer cars, safer roads: New rules take effect
content: Since 7 July 2026, newly registered cars and vans are subject to more advanced safety requirements
snippet: Since 7 July 2026, newly registered cars and vans are subject to more advanced safety requirements

title: Commission seeks feedback on commitments offered by Sanofi over possible anticompetitive conduct regarding the promotion of a flu vaccine for vulnerable patients
content: The European Commission invites comments on commitments offered by Sanofi to address competition concerns regarding a communication campaign that has possibly disparaged the only rival flu vaccine recommended for vulnerable patients with risk factors.
snippet: The European Commission invites comments on commitments offered by Sanofi to address competition concerns regarding a communication campaign that has possibly disparaged the only rival flu vaccine

title: EU and UK: a post-Brexit milestone on Election Observation Missions (EOMs)
content: The European Union and the United Kingdom have signed an administrative arrangement allowing British observers to take part once again in EU Election Observation Missions (EOMs) in third countries.
snippet: The European Union and the United Kingdom have signed an administrative arrangement allowing British observers to take part once again in EU Election Observation Missions (EOMs) in third countries.

title: New EU plan to address the risks and opportunities of advanced AI for cybersecurity
content: The European Commission has presented a plan to address the risks and harness the opportunities of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity.
snippet: The European Commission has presented a plan to address the risks and harness the opportunities of advanced artificial intelligence (AI) in cybersecurity.

title: Setting sustainable livestock farming on a strong footing
content: The European Commission has introduced a new strategy for the livestock sector to ensure that farmers can address economic and environmental challenges sustainably in the long term.
snippet: The European Commission has introduced a new strategy for the livestock sector to ensure that farmers can address economic and environmental challenges sustainably in the long term.

title: Interconnections for South-West Europe at core of Ministerial Meeting in Paris today
content: The South-West High-Level Group on Interconnections for South-West Europe met in Paris today, bringing together energy ministers of France, Spain, Portugal and the European Commission, to advance on key Trans-Pyrenean interconnection projects, offshore energy and hydrogen.

title: Portugal achieves major milestone with permit for offshore aquaculture facility
content: Mariculture Systems, a company based in Ramat Negev, Israel, has announced that it has obtained an Aquaculture Activity Title (Ttulo de Atividade Aqucola - TAA) from the Portuguese authorities.
snippet: Mariculture Systems, a company based in Ramat Negev, Israel, has announced that it has obtained an Aquaculture Activity Title (Ttulo de Atividade Aqucola - TAA) from the Portuguese authorities.

title: Five joint defence projects to strengthen Europe's industrial capabilities
content: The Commission has proposed five large-scale European Defence Projects of Common Interest (EDPCIs), to help EU countries strengthen their defence capabilities by developing key military systems together. It has allocated €325 million to support the establishment and deployment of these projects.
snippet: The Commission has proposed five large-scale European Defence Projects of Common Interest (EDPCIs), to help EU countries strengthen their defence capabilities by developing key military systems

title: Quarterly reports show a surge in solar energy and e-vehicles, with record highs in 2025
content: The latest quarterly reports for the electricity and gas markets, covering the final two quarters of 2025, confirm continued growth in the generation of renewable energy in the EU.
snippet: The latest quarterly reports for the electricity and gas markets, covering the final two quarters of 2025, confirm continued growth in the generation of renewable energy in the EU.

title: Short-term outlook of EU agricultural markets: EU Agriculture remains robust amidst uncertainties and rising input costs
content: Based on the latest short-term outlook report, EU agricultural markets remain resilient in 2026 despite conflicts, weather risks and rising input costs. Production is set to grow in key sectors, supported by new market outlook insights.

title: Commissioner Šuica travels to Cairo to strengthen euro-mediterranean parliamentary cooperation and youth engagement
content: Tomorrow and Sunday, Commissioner for the Mediterranean Dubravka Šuica will be in Cairo, Egypt, to participate in the 10th Speakers' Summit of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Union for the Mediterranean (PA-UfM).

title: Europe’s job market is shifting, and EURES is ready to lead the way into 2030
content: The new EURES strategy sets a clear direction to make finding work and workforce needs easier, fairer and more connected across Europe.
snippet: The new EURES strategy sets a clear direction to make finding work and workforce needs easier, fairer and more connected across Europe.

title: Commission proposes five joint defence projects to strengthen Europe's industrial capabilities
content: Today, on 3rd July 2026, the European Commission proposed five new large-scale projects, known as the European Defence Projects of Common Interest (EDPCIs), to help EU countries strengthen their defence capabilities by developing key military systems together.

title: Commission seeks feedback on targeted amendment of EMFAF
content: The European Commission is seeking feedback in view of a possible targeted amendment of the European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund (EMFAF).
snippet: The European Commission is seeking feedback in view of a possible targeted amendment of the European Maritime, Fisheries and Aquaculture Fund (EMFAF).

title: Commissioner Kadis hosts implementation dialogue on EU aquaculture policy
content: Costas Kadis, Commissioner for Fisheries and Oceans, hosted an implementation dialogue on EU aquaculture policy, which brought together stakeholders from across the sector to discuss how the European Union can unlock the full potential of sustainable aquaculture.

title: Internal Audit in Motion: Keeping Pace with a Changing Europe
content: The European Commission’s Internal Audit Service (IAS) will host its annual conference on 11 September 2026, in Brussels at the Charlemagne building.
snippet: The European Commission’s Internal Audit Service (IAS) will host its annual conference on 11 September 2026, in Brussels at the Charlemagne building.

title: Commission adopts revised sustainability reporting standards to reduce administrative burdens for EU businesses while maintaining high-quality disclosures
content: The revised standards adopted today are designed to reduce administrative burdens for EU businesses while maintaining high‑quality disclosures.
snippet: The revised standards adopted today are designed to reduce administrative burdens for EU businesses while maintaining high‑quality disclosures.

title: Timing of the publication of the EU ETS auction calendar
content: The European Energy Exchange (EEX) will publish the revised 2026 and the new 2027 auction calendars for allowances in respect of stationary installations, maritime transport and aviation (ETS1 allowances) before 31 July.

title: The Commission advances work on restrictions of hazardous chemical substances
content: Today, the European Commission presented a revised rolling list of its Restrictions Roadmap under REACH, the EU’s comprehensive chemicals legislation governing the registration, evaluation, authorisation, and restriction of chemicals, protecting human health and the environment from chemical risks.

title: 25th PES Meeting: Board discusses key EU initiatives, PES contribution to the Union of Skills, Labour mobility and collaborative aspects of digital tools
content: During the 25th Board Meeting of the European Network of Public Employment Services (PES Network), hosted by the Cypriot Presidency on 18-19 June 2026 in Nicosia, Heads of PES came together to discuss the role of Public Employment Services in key EU initiatives and how PES can translate the strategi

title: Joint press release: €2.5 billion of EU ETS revenues invested in cleaner energy systems in 11 EU countries
content: The European Commission and the European Investment Bank have announced today the disbursement of €2.5 billion from the Modernisation Fund to support 51 energy-related projects in 11 EU Member States.
snippet: The European Commission and the European Investment Bank have announced today the disbursement of €2.5 billion from the Modernisation Fund to support 51 energy-related projects in 11 EU Member States.

title: From smarter systems to greener practices, how European businesses are embracing a sustainable digital future
content: How is sustainability being integrated into Europe’s digitalisation efforts? The EU has just released statistics that indicate a promising trend.
snippet: How is sustainability being integrated into Europe’s digitalisation efforts? The EU has just released statistics that indicate a promising trend.

title: 9th Seminar of European Customs Chemists
content: The 9th Seminar of European Customs Chemists (9SECC) took place in Stockholm, Sweden, from 1 to 3 July 2026 under the theme ‘Fostering Unity in Uncertain Times’.
snippet: The 9th Seminar of European Customs Chemists (9SECC) took place in Stockholm, Sweden, from 1 to 3 July 2026 under the theme ‘Fostering Unity in Uncertain Times’.

title: Farmers, fair markets and consumers: new report highlights the role of EU competition rules in agriculture
content: The Commission published its periodic report on the application of EU competition rules in the agricultural sector. The report shows that these rules give farmers opportunities to work together, strengthen their position and contribute to fairer agri-food markets.

title: EU and South Africa kick off intergovernmental dialogue on Clean Trade and Investment Partnership
content: Today, the EU and the South Africa are holding the first government-to-government dialogue at senior-official level to advance on the implementation of their Clean Trade and Investment Partnership (CTIP).

title: Commission imposes anti-dumping duties on passenger car and light lorry tyres from China
content: On 7 July 2026, the Commission published a regulation imposing definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of passenger car and light lorry tyres originating in the People’s Republic of China.
snippet: On 7 July 2026, the Commission published a regulation imposing definitive anti-dumping duties on imports of passenger car and light lorry tyres originating in the People’s Republic of China.

title: Building a circular economy with the EU Ecolabel
content: Europe's circular economy transition is changing what is expected from products and businesses. How does the EU Ecolabel act as a practical tool for linking policy objectives to everyday market choices?

title: Open Door Day at Koksijde Air Base showcases how Galileo supports Search and Rescue operations
content: On 8 June, around 10,000 visitors gathered at Koksijde Air Base in Belgium to celebrate 80 years of the Belgian Air Force and 65 years of the Search and Rescue Service (SAR). title: From a remote island to shelves around the world: the story of Azorean canned fish
content: The island of Terceira in the Azores sits 1,500 km west of mainland Portugal – remote enough to make running a business complicated, let alone one built around delivering fresh fish to distant markets. Tibério Barbeito started TZT Flying Fish Azores in his twenties with exactly that ambition.

title: International Plastic Bag Free Day – from single use to reuse
content: On the occasion of International Plastic Bag Free Day, take a look at what the EU is doing to eliminate the use of plastic bags and disposable plastics more generally, and to promote eco-friendly reusable alternatives.

title: International cooperation in language services: DG Translation at IAMLADP 2026
content: DG Translation works with international organisations through networks such as IAMLADP and ISO to share expertise, develop common approaches and support high-quality multilingual services.
snippet: DG Translation works with international organisations through networks such as IAMLADP and ISO to share expertise, develop common approaches and support high-quality multilingual services.
